configuration-properties.doc:name: Configuration Properties
configuration-properties.doc:description: Loads HTTP host, port and TLS keystore values from config.yaml

http:listener-config.name: httpListenerConfig
http:listener-config.doc:name: HTTP Listener Config
http:listener-config.doc:description: HTTPS Listener bound to 0.0.0.0:8081 serving all three resource API endpoints

tls:context.doc:name: Local TLS Context
tls:context.doc:description: Self-signed TLS context for local HTTPS development on localhost

flow.name: get-resources-flow
flow.doc:description: Handles GET /resources and returns the complete hardcoded JSON array of resources

http:listener.doc:name: GET /resources
http:listener.doc:description: Listens for GET requests on /resources and returns all available resource records

ee:transform.doc:name: Return all resources
ee:transform.doc:description: Produces the hardcoded JSON array of resource objects as the response payload

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- [ { id: "1", name: "Resource Alpha", "type": "compute", status: "active" }, { id: "2", name: "Resource Beta", "type": "storage", status: "inactive" }, { id: "3", name: "Resource Gamma", "type": "network", status: "active" } ]

logger.message: #['GET /resources — returned ' ++ (sizeOf(payload as Array<Any>) as String) ++ ' items']
logger.doc:name: Log response
logger.doc:description: Logs the number of resource items returned in the GET /resources response

flow.name: get-resource-by-id-flow
flow.doc:description: Handles GET /resources/{id}, filters the hardcoded array by ID, and returns the matched resource or 404

http:listener.doc:name: GET /resources/{id}
http:listener.doc:description: Listens for GET requests on /resources/{id} to retrieve a specific resource by its ID

ee:transform.doc:name: Filter resources by ID
ee:transform.doc:description: Searches the hardcoded resources array for an entry whose id matches the incoming URI parameter

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json var allResources = [ { id: "1", name: "Resource Alpha", "type": "compute", status: "active" }, { id: "2", name: "Resource Beta", "type": "storage", status: "inactive" }, { id: "3", name: "Resource Gamma", "type": "network", status: "active" } ] --- allResources filter ($.id == attributes.uriParams.id)

choice.doc:name: Resource found?
choice.doc:description: Routes the flow based on whether the filter returned a matching resource

when.doc:name: Match found
when.doc:description: At least one resource matched the requested ID — return it with HTTP 200

set-variable.doc:name: Set HTTP 200
set-variable.doc:description: Sets the HTTP response status code to 200 OK for a successful resource lookup

ee:transform.doc:name: Extract matched resource
ee:transform.doc:description: Extracts the first matched resource object from the filtered array for the response payload

otherwise.doc:name: No match
otherwise.doc:description: No resource matched the requested ID — return a structured JSON error with HTTP 404

set-variable.doc:name: Set HTTP 404
set-variable.doc:description: Sets the HTTP response status code to 404 Not Found when the resource ID does not exist

ee:transform.doc:name: Build 404 error body
ee:transform.doc:description: Constructs a JSON error payload indicating the requested resource was not found

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- { error: "Resource not found", id: attributes.uriParams.id }

logger.doc:name: Log lookup result
logger.doc:description: Logs the resource ID that was looked up and the HTTP status code of the response

flow.name: post-resource-flow
flow.doc:description: Handles POST /resources, accepts a JSON body, and responds with a 201 Created success message

http:listener.doc:name: POST /resources
http:listener.doc:description: Listens for POST requests on /resources to accept a new resource payload

ee:transform.doc:name: Build 201 success response
ee:transform.doc:description: Returns a structured JSON success message with status 201 and an echo of the received payload

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- { message: "Resource created successfully", status: 201, receivedPayload: payload }

logger.message: POST /resources — resource creation request processed with HTTP 201 Created
logger.doc:name: Log creation
logger.doc:description: Logs successful processing of the POST /resources request with the 201 Created status
